19	// NowFunc allows tests to override time.Now for deterministic PreserveRef output.
20	var NowFunc = time.Now
21	
22	// PreserveRef builds the documented hidden ref for a preserved iteration.
23	func PreserveRef(beadID, baseRev string) string {
24		shortSHA := baseRev
25		if len(shortSHA) > 12 {
26			shortSHA = shortSHA[:12]
27		}
28		timestamp := NowFunc().UTC().Format("20060102T150405Z")
29		return fmt.Sprintf("refs/ddx/iterations/%s/%s-%s", beadID, timestamp, shortSHA)
30	}
31	
32	// GateCheckResult records the outcome of one required execution gate.
33	type GateCheckResult struct {
34		DefinitionID string `json:"definition_id"`
35		Required     bool   `json:"required"`
36		ExitCode     int    `json:"exit_code"`
37		// Status is "pass", "fail", or "skipped".
38		Status string `json:"status"`
39		Stdout string `json:"stdout,omitempty"`
40		Stderr string `json:"stderr,omitempty"`
41		// Ratchet captures the ratchet evaluation when the gate declared
42		// thresholds. Populated for both pass and fail decisions so HELIX can
43		// distinguish ratchet outcomes from generic command failures.
44		Ratchet *RatchetEvidence `json:"ratchet,omitempty"`
45	}
46	
47	// RatchetEvidence is the machine-readable record of one ratchet evaluation
48	// performed before landing. Threshold/Observed carry the authored ratchet
49	// and the observed value; Decision is "pass" or "fail"; Reason provides a
50	// short explanation of what was compared ("observed 310 ms > ratchet 250 ms").
51	type RatchetEvidence struct {
52		DefinitionID string  `json:"definition_id"`
53		MetricID     string  `json:"metric_id,omitempty"`
54		Comparison   string  `json:"comparison,omitempty"`
55		Threshold    float64 `json:"threshold"`
56		Observed     float64 `json:"observed"`
57		Unit         string  `json:"unit,omitempty"`
58		Decision     string  `json:"decision"`
59		Reason       string  `json:"reason,omitempty"`
60	}
61	
62	// RatchetPreserveReason marks a landing that was preserved because a declared
63	// ratchet was not met. Callers match on this exact string (via the landing
64	// result's Reason field) to bucket ratchet-preserved attempts apart from
65	// generic gate failures.
66	const RatchetPreserveReason = "ratchet miss"
67	
68	// executeBeadChecks is the machine-readable schema for checks.json.
69	// Written by the orchestrator when gate evaluation runs.
70	type executeBeadChecks struct {
71		AttemptID       string            `json:"attempt_id"`
72		EvaluatedAt     time.Time         `json:"evaluated_at"`
73		Summary         string            `json:"summary"`
74		Results         []GateCheckResult `json:"results"`
75		RatchetSummary  string            `json:"ratchet_summary,omitempty"`
76		RatchetEvidence []RatchetEvidence `json:"ratchet_evidence,omitempty"`
77	}
78	
79	// OrchestratorGitOps abstracts the git operations needed by the parent-side
80	// orchestrator for preserving worker results under iteration refs.
81	//
82	// NOTE: The Merge(dir, rev) method that existed here before the land
83	// coordinator redesign has been DELETED. All target-branch writes now flow
84	// through Land() in execute_bead_land.go and its per-project serialized
85	// coordinator. See ddx-8746d8a6 / ddx-e14efc58 / ddx-6aa50e57 for the
86	// rationale: the old path produced "chore: checkpoint before merge" noise
87	// and workers racing on the same projectRoot could corrupt each other's
88	// intermediate state. Land() serializes through a single goroutine and
89	// uses `git merge --no-ff` when the target has advanced, so the worker's
90	// commit is never rewritten and replay sees the same inputs it originally saw.
91	//
92	// The LandingAdvancer field on BeadLandingOptions is the coordinator
93	// injection point for LandBeadResult callers that need to ff the target
94	// branch. When LandingAdvancer is nil (the interactive single-bead CLI),
95	// LandBeadResult falls back to preserving the result under
96	// refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/... rather than modifying the target branch.
97	type OrchestratorGitOps interface {
98		UpdateRef(dir, ref, sha string) error
99	}
100	
101	// RealOrchestratorGitOps implements OrchestratorGitOps via os/exec git commands.
102	type RealOrchestratorGitOps struct{}
103	
104	// UpdateRef updates a git ref to point at sha.
105	func (r *RealOrchestratorGitOps) UpdateRef(dir, ref, sha string) error {
106		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "update-ref", ref, sha).CombinedOutput()
107		if err != nil {
108			return fmt.Errorf("git update-ref %s: %s: %w", ref, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
109		}
110		return nil
111	}
112	
113	// BeadLandingOptions controls how the orchestrator lands a completed worker result.
114	type BeadLandingOptions struct {
115		// NoMerge skips the land step and preserves the result under
116		// refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/... instead.
117		NoMerge bool
118	
119		// WtPath is the path to a worktree checked out at ResultRev, used for gate
120		// evaluation. When empty, gate evaluation is skipped (the worktree has
121		// typically been cleaned up by the time the orchestrator runs).
122		WtPath string
123	
124		// GovernIDs are the governing artifact IDs to use for gate evaluation.
125		// Only used when WtPath is non-empty. Typically extracted from the worker's
126		// manifest artifact via ExtractGoverningIDsFromManifest.
127		GovernIDs []string
128	
129		// ChecksArtifactPath is the absolute path to write checks.json. Optional.
130		ChecksArtifactPath string
131		// ChecksArtifactRel is the relative path stored in the result for checks.json.
132		ChecksArtifactRel string
133	
134		// LandingAdvancer, when non-nil, replaces the old in-process Merge step
135		// with the coordinator-pattern Land() call. The callback is expected to
136		// run fetch → (ff or merge) → push serialized against other submissions
137		// for the same projectRoot. When nil, LandBeadResult falls back to
138		// preserving the result under refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/...
139		// rather than touching the target branch.
140		LandingAdvancer func(res *ExecuteBeadResult) (*LandResult, error)
141	}
142	
143	// BeadLandingResult records the outcome of the orchestrator's landing step.
144	type BeadLandingResult struct {
145		// Outcome is one of: "merged", "preserved", "no-changes".
146		Outcome string `json:"outcome"`
147		// Reason is a human-readable explanation of the outcome.
148		Reason string `json:"reason,omitempty"`
149		// PreserveRef is set when the result was preserved under refs/ddx/iterations/...
150		PreserveRef string `json:"preserve_ref,omitempty"`
151		// GateResults holds the outcome of each required execution gate.
152		GateResults []GateCheckResult `json:"gate_results,omitempty"`
153		// RequiredExecSummary is "pass", "fail", or "skipped".
154		RequiredExecSummary string `json:"required_exec_summary,omitempty"`
155		// ChecksFile is the relative path to checks.json when gate results were written.
156		ChecksFile string `json:"checks_file,omitempty"`
157		// RatchetEvidence aggregates every ratchet-evaluated gate's evidence for
158		// the attempt. Surfaced separately from GateResults so HELIX and other
159		// consumers can tell apart ratchet outcomes from generic gate failures.
160		RatchetEvidence []RatchetEvidence `json:"ratchet_evidence,omitempty"`
161		// RatchetSummary is "pass", "fail", or "" (no ratchets evaluated).
162		RatchetSummary string `json:"ratchet_summary,omitempty"`
163	}
164	
165	// LandBeadResult is the parent-side orchestrator. It receives a completed worker
166	// result and decides whether to merge, preserve, or report no-changes. All
167	// Merge, UpdateRef, gate evaluation, and preserve-ref management live here.
168	// The worker (ExecuteBead) must not call any of these operations.
169	//
170	// Outcome rules:
171	//   - ResultRev == BaseRev → "no-changes" (agent made no commits)
172	//   - ExitCode != 0 with commits → "preserved" (agent failed but left output)
173	//   - Gate failed → "preserved"
174	//   - NoMerge → "preserved"
175	//   - Default → attempt merge; on conflict → "preserved"
176	func LandBeadResult(projectRoot string, res *ExecuteBeadResult, gitOps OrchestratorGitOps, opts BeadLandingOptions) (*BeadLandingResult, error) {
177		landing := &BeadLandingResult{}
178	
179		// Agent failed with no commits: report as error (not no-changes).
180		// Prefer res.Reason (e.g. HeadRev failure set by worker) over res.Error
181		// (agent error) so the primary context failure is surfaced as the reason.
182		agentFailed := res.ExitCode != 0 || res.Outcome == ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed
183		if agentFailed && res.ResultRev == res.BaseRev {
184			landing.Outcome = "error"
185			switch {
186			case res.Reason != "":
187				landing.Reason = res.Reason
188			case res.Error != "":
189				landing.Reason = res.Error
190			default:
191				landing.Reason = "agent execution failed"
192			}
193			return landing, nil
194		}
195	
196		// No changes from the worker: nothing to land.
197		if res.ResultRev == res.BaseRev {
198			if res.Outcome == ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence || res.FailureMode == FailureModeNoEvidenceProduced {
199				landing.Outcome = "no-evidence"
200				if res.Reason != "" {
201					landing.Reason = res.Reason
202				} else if res.Error != "" {
203					landing.Reason = res.Error
204				} else {
205					landing.Reason = "agent exited without a commit or no_changes_rationale.txt"
206				}
207				return landing, nil
208			}
209			landing.Outcome = "no-changes"
210			if res.NoChangesRationale != "" {
211				landing.Reason = res.NoChangesRationale
212			} else {
213				landing.Reason = "agent made no commits"
214			}
215			return landing, nil
216		}
217	
218		// Agent failed but produced commits: preserve without attempting merge.
219		if agentFailed {
220			ref := PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
221			if err := gitOps.UpdateRef(projectRoot, ref, res.ResultRev); err != nil {
222				return nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving result ref: %w", err)
223			}
224			landing.Outcome = "preserved"
225			landing.PreserveRef = ref
226			landing.Reason = "agent execution failed"
227			return landing, nil
228		}
229	
230		// Evaluate required gates when a worktree path and governing IDs are
231		// provided. EvaluateRequiredGatesForResult is the canonical helper —
232		// shared with upstream callers that want gate eval before submitting
233		// through the coordinator. It mutates res.GateResults / RequiredExecSummary
234		// / RatchetEvidence / RatchetSummary / ChecksFile in place; we mirror the
235		// outputs onto landing so ApplyLandingToResult can copy them back later
236		// without losing the existing landing.* contract.
237		anyGateFailed, anyRatchetFailed, gateErr := EvaluateRequiredGatesForResult(
238			opts.WtPath, opts.GovernIDs, res, projectRoot,
239			opts.ChecksArtifactPath, opts.ChecksArtifactRel,
240		)
241		if gateErr != nil {
242			return nil, gateErr
243		}
244		gateResults := res.GateResults
245		landing.GateResults = gateResults
246		landing.RequiredExecSummary = res.RequiredExecSummary
247		landing.RatchetEvidence = res.RatchetEvidence
248		landing.RatchetSummary = res.RatchetSummary
249		landing.ChecksFile = res.ChecksFile
250	
251		// Gate failed: preserve instead of merging. Ratchet misses get a
252		// dedicated reason so status/failure_mode classifiers can distinguish
253		// them from generic command failures.
254		if anyGateFailed {
255			ref := PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
256			if err := gitOps.UpdateRef(projectRoot, ref, res.ResultRev); err != nil {
257				return nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving result ref: %w", err)
258			}
259			landing.Outcome = "preserved"
260			landing.PreserveRef = ref
261			if anyRatchetFailed {
262				landing.Reason = RatchetPreserveReason
263			} else {
264				landing.Reason = "post-run checks failed"
265			}
266			return landing, nil
267		}
268	
269		// --no-merge: preserve unconditionally.
270		if opts.NoMerge {
271			ref := PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
272			if err := gitOps.UpdateRef(projectRoot, ref, res.ResultRev); err != nil {
273				return nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving result ref: %w", err)
274			}
275			landing.Outcome = "preserved"
276			landing.PreserveRef = ref
277			landing.Reason = "--no-merge specified"
278			return landing, nil
279		}
280	
281		// Default: land the worker's commits on the target branch. When a
282		// LandingAdvancer is provided (server coordinator / --local coordinator)
283		// it runs the fetch → (ff or merge) → push sequence serialized per
284		// projectRoot. When no advancer is provided, LandBeadResult falls back
285		// to preserving the result under refs/ddx/iterations/ — the interactive
286		// single-bead CLI path, which intentionally does NOT auto-advance the
287		// target branch (the operator moves the ref themselves).
288		if opts.LandingAdvancer != nil {
289			land, landErr := opts.LandingAdvancer(res)
290			if landErr != nil {
291				return nil, fmt.Errorf("land advancer: %w", landErr)
292			}
293			switch land.Status {
294			case "landed":
295				landing.Outcome = "merged"
296				if land.Merged {
297					landing.Reason = "merged onto current tip"
298				}
299				if land.NewTip != "" {
300					res.ResultRev = land.NewTip
301				}
302				if land.PushFailed {
303					landing.Reason = PushFailedReasonPrefix + " " + land.PushError
304				}
305			case "preserved":
306				landing.Outcome = "preserved"
307				landing.PreserveRef = land.PreserveRef
308				landing.Reason = land.Reason
309			case "no-changes":
310				landing.Outcome = "no-changes"
311				landing.Reason = land.Reason
312			default:
313				landing.Outcome = "preserved"
314				landing.Reason = "unknown land status: " + land.Status
315			}
316			return landing, nil
317		}
318	
319		// No advancer: preserve under refs/ddx/iterations/ as a safe fallback.
320		ref := PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
321		if err := gitOps.UpdateRef(projectRoot, ref, res.ResultRev); err != nil {
322			return nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving result ref (no land advancer): %w", err)
323		}
324		landing.Outcome = "preserved"
325		landing.PreserveRef = ref
326		landing.Reason = "no land advancer configured"
327		return landing, nil
328	}
329	
330	// ApplyLandingToResult merges a BeadLandingResult's fields into an
331	// ExecuteBeadResult so callers can output a single unified record. It
332	// overwrites Outcome, Status, Detail, Reason, PreserveRef, GateResults,
333	// RequiredExecSummary, and ChecksFile based on the landing decision.
334	func ApplyLandingToResult(res *ExecuteBeadResult, landing *BeadLandingResult) {
335		res.Outcome = landing.Outcome
336		res.Reason = landing.Reason
337		res.PreserveRef = landing.PreserveRef
338		res.GateResults = landing.GateResults
339		res.RequiredExecSummary = landing.RequiredExecSummary
340		res.ChecksFile = landing.ChecksFile
341		res.RatchetEvidence = landing.RatchetEvidence
342		res.RatchetSummary = landing.RatchetSummary
343		// Re-classify status based on landing outcome and reason.
344		res.Status = ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(landing.Outcome, res.ExitCode, landing.Reason)
345		res.Detail = ExecuteBeadStatusDetail(res.Status, landing.Reason, res.Error)
346		// Refine failure_mode with landing-level signals. A clean merge clears
347		// the field; merge conflict or gate failure overrides the worker-level
348		// classification; other preserved outcomes keep the worker's mode.
349		res.FailureMode = classifyLandingFailureMode(landing.Outcome, landing.Reason, landing.GateResults, res.FailureMode)
350		res.OrchestratorStatus = res.Status
351	}
352	
353	// ExtractGoverningIDsFromManifest reads governing artifact IDs from a manifest
354	// JSON file at manifestAbs (absolute path). Returns nil when the file cannot
355	// be read. Callers use this to populate BeadLandingOptions.GovernIDs.
356	func ExtractGoverningIDsFromManifest(manifestAbs string) []string {
357		if manifestAbs == "" {
358			return nil
359		}
360		type manifestShape struct {
361			Governing []struct {
362				ID string `json:"id"`
363			} `json:"governing"`
364		}
365		raw, err := os.ReadFile(manifestAbs)
366		if err != nil {
367			return nil
368		}
369		var m manifestShape
370		if err := json.Unmarshal(raw, &m); err != nil {
371			return nil
372		}
373		ids := make([]string, 0, len(m.Governing))
374		for _, g := range m.Governing {
375			if g.ID != "" {
376				ids = append(ids, g.ID)
377			}
378		}
379		return ids
380	}
381	
382	// RecoverOrphans removes orphaned execute-bead worktrees for a given bead ID.
383	// This is the parent's responsibility — call it before spawning new workers
384	// so stale worktrees from crashed previous attempts do not accumulate.
385	func RecoverOrphans(gitOps GitOps, workDir, beadID string) {
386		paths, err := gitOps.WorktreeList(workDir)
387		if err != nil {
388			return
389		}
390		// Match by basename prefix so orphans are found regardless of their parent
391		// directory (legacy .ddx/ vs new $TMPDIR/ddx-exec-wt/).
392		basenamePrefix := ExecuteBeadWtPrefix + beadID + "-"
393		orphanRemoved := false
394		for _, p := range paths {
395			if strings.HasPrefix(filepath.Base(p), basenamePrefix) {
396				_ = gitOps.WorktreeRemove(workDir, p)
397				orphanRemoved = true
398			}
399		}
400		_ = gitOps.WorktreePrune(workDir)
401	
402		// Sweep stale run-state left by crashed workers. Per-attempt records are
403		// cleared individually so concurrent attempts for other beads remain visible.
404		if states, _ := ReadRunStates(workDir); len(states) > 0 {
405			for _, state := range states {
406				remove := orphanRemoved && state.BeadID == beadID
407				if !remove && state.BeadID == beadID {
408					remove = true
409				}
410				if !remove && state.WorktreePath != "" {
411					if _, err := os.Stat(state.WorktreePath); os.IsNotExist(err) {
412						remove = true
413					}
414				}
415				if remove {
416					_ = ClearRunStateAttempt(workDir, state.AttemptID)
417				}
418			}
419		}
420	}
421	
422	// evaluateRequiredGates resolves graph-authored execution documents that are
423	// required and linked to any of the governing artifact IDs, then runs each
424	// one. When a gate declares a ratchet threshold, the command's numeric output
425	// is compared against the threshold after a successful exit; a ratchet miss
426	// demotes the gate status to "fail" and records the evidence the orchestrator
427	// surfaces as part of its landing decision.
428	//
429	// Returns: gate results (one per candidate), anyFailed (true if any gate's
430	// final status is "fail"), anyRatchetFailed (true if any failure was caused
431	// by a ratchet miss rather than a non-zero exit), and any fatal error from
432	// walking the graph (soft errors are swallowed).
433	func evaluateRequiredGates(wtPath string, governingIDs []string) ([]GateCheckResult, bool, bool, error) {
434		if len(governingIDs) == 0 {
435			return nil, false, false, nil
436		}
437	
438		graph, err := docgraph.BuildGraphWithConfig(wtPath)
439		if err != nil {
440			// Soft error: skip gate evaluation rather than blocking all landings.
441			return nil, false, false, nil
442		}
443	
444		governingSet := make(map[string]bool, len(governingIDs))
445		for _, id := range governingIDs {
446			governingSet[id] = true
447		}
448	
449		type execCandidate struct {
450			id         string
451			command    []string
452			cwd        string
453			comparison string
454			thresholds *docgraph.DocThresholds
455			metric     *docgraph.DocMetricSpec
456		}
457		var candidates []execCandidate
458		for _, doc := range graph.Documents {
459			if doc.ExecDef == nil || !doc.ExecDef.Required {
460				continue
461			}
462			ed := doc.ExecDef
463			if ed.Kind != "command" {
464				continue
465			}
466			if len(ed.Command) == 0 {
467				continue
468			}
469			linked := false
470			for _, dep := range doc.DependsOn {
471				if governingSet[dep] {
472					linked = true
473					break
474				}
475			}
476			if !linked {
477				for _, artID := range ed.ArtifactIDs {
478					if governingSet[artID] {
479						linked = true
480						break
481					}
482				}
483			}
484			if !linked {
485				continue
486			}
487			candidates = append(candidates, execCandidate{
488				id:         doc.ID,
489				command:    ed.Command,
490				cwd:        ed.Cwd,
491				comparison: ed.Comparison,
492				thresholds: ed.Thresholds,
493				metric:     ed.Metric,
494			})
495		}
496	
497		if len(candidates) == 0 {
498			return nil, false, false, nil
499		}
500	
501		anyFailed := false
502		anyRatchetFailed := false
503		results := make([]GateCheckResult, 0, len(candidates))
504		for _, c := range candidates {
505			cwd := wtPath
506			if c.cwd != "" {
507				if filepath.IsAbs(c.cwd) {
508					cwd = c.cwd
509				} else {
510					cwd = filepath.Join(wtPath, c.cwd)
511				}
512			}
513	
514			ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Minute)
515			cmd := osexec.CommandContext(ctx, c.command[0], c.command[1:]...)
516			cmd.Dir = cwd
517			var stdoutBuf, stderrBuf strings.Builder
518			cmd.Stdout = &stdoutBuf
519			cmd.Stderr = &stderrBuf
520			runErr := cmd.Run()
521			cancel()
522	
523			stdoutStr := strings.TrimSpace(stdoutBuf.String())
524			stderrStr := strings.TrimSpace(stderrBuf.String())
525			gr := GateCheckResult{
526				DefinitionID: c.id,
527				Required:     true,
528				Stdout:       stdoutStr,
529				Stderr:       stderrStr,
530			}
531			if runErr != nil {
532				gr.ExitCode = 1
533				if exitErr, ok := runErr.(*osexec.ExitError); ok {
534					gr.ExitCode = exitErr.ExitCode()
535				}
536				gr.Status = "fail"
537				anyFailed = true
538			} else {
539				gr.Status = "pass"
540			}
541	
542			// Apply ratchet evaluation when thresholds are declared. Only runs
543			// when the command exited cleanly — a non-zero exit already failed
544			// the gate and there's no point parsing unreliable output.
545			if c.thresholds != nil && gr.Status == "pass" {
546				evidence := evaluateRatchet(c.id, c.comparison, c.thresholds, c.metric, stdoutStr, stderrStr)
547				gr.Ratchet = evidence
548				if evidence.Decision == "fail" {
549					gr.Status = "fail"
550					anyFailed = true
551					anyRatchetFailed = true
552				}
553			}
554			results = append(results, gr)
555		}
556	
557		return results, anyFailed, anyRatchetFailed, nil
558	}
559	
560	// evaluateRatchet parses the gate's observed value from stdout and compares it
561	// against the declared ratchet threshold. Always returns a non-nil evidence
562	// record so pass/fail results both carry machine-readable provenance.
563	func evaluateRatchet(definitionID, comparison string, thresholds *docgraph.DocThresholds, metric *docgraph.DocMetricSpec, stdout, stderr string) *RatchetEvidence {
564		if comparison == "" {
565			comparison = "lower-is-better"
566		}
567		unit := ""
568		metricID := ""
569		if metric != nil {
570			unit = metric.Unit
571			metricID = metric.MetricID
572		}
573		if unit == "" {
574			unit = thresholds.Unit
575		}
576		observed, parsedUnit, parsed := parseGateValue(stdout)
577		if !parsed {
578			// Fall back to stderr for tools that report numbers there.
579			observed, parsedUnit, parsed = parseGateValue(stderr)
580		}
581		if unit == "" {
582			unit = parsedUnit
583		}
584		evidence := &RatchetEvidence{
585			DefinitionID: definitionID,
586			MetricID:     metricID,
587			Comparison:   comparison,
588			Threshold:    thresholds.Ratchet,
589			Observed:     observed,
590			Unit:         unit,
591		}
592		if !parsed {
593			evidence.Decision = "fail"
594			evidence.Reason = "gate did not emit a parseable numeric value"
595			return evidence
596		}
597		passed := ratchetPasses(comparison, thresholds.Ratchet, observed)
598		if passed {
599			evidence.Decision = "pass"
600			evidence.Reason = formatRatchetReason(comparison, thresholds.Ratchet, observed, unit, true)
601		} else {
602			evidence.Decision = "fail"
603			evidence.Reason = formatRatchetReason(comparison, thresholds.Ratchet, observed, unit, false)
604		}
605		return evidence
606	}
607	
608	// ratchetPasses returns true when observed satisfies the ratchet constraint.
609	// lower-is-better: observed must be <= threshold. higher-is-better: observed
610	// must be >= threshold. Any other comparison string defaults to lower-is-better.
611	func ratchetPasses(comparison string, threshold, observed float64) bool {
612		if comparison == "higher-is-better" {
613			return observed >= threshold
614		}
615		return observed <= threshold
616	}
617	
618	func formatRatchetReason(comparison string, threshold, observed float64, unit string, passed bool) string {
619		cmpOp := "<="
620		if comparison == "higher-is-better" {
621			cmpOp = ">="
622		}
623		state := "pass"
624		if !passed {
625			state = "fail"
626			// flip operator for failures so the message reads like "observed 310 > ratchet 250"
627			if comparison == "higher-is-better" {
628				cmpOp = "<"
629			} else {
630				cmpOp = ">"
631			}
632		}
633		if unit != "" {
634			return fmt.Sprintf("%s: observed %g %s %s ratchet %g %s", state, observed, unit, cmpOp, threshold, unit)
635		}
636		return fmt.Sprintf("%s: observed %g %s ratchet %g", state, observed, cmpOp, threshold)
637	}
638	
639	// parseGateValue extracts a numeric observation from gate output. Accepts
640	// either a JSON object with a "value" (and optional "unit") field, or a
641	// trailing number with an optional unit suffix. Returns the value, the parsed
642	// unit (may be ""), and whether parsing succeeded.
643	func parseGateValue(text string) (float64, string, bool) {
644		text = strings.TrimSpace(text)
645		if text == "" {
646			return 0, "", false
647		}
648		if v, u, ok := parseGateJSON(text); ok {
649			return v, u, true
650		}
651		return parseGateText(text)
652	}
653	
654	var gateMeasurementPattern = regexp.MustCompile(`(-?\d+(?:\.\d+)?)(?:\s*)([a-zA-Z%/]+)?`)
655	
656	func parseGateJSON(text string) (float64, string, bool) {
657		var obj map[string]any
658		if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(text), &obj); err != nil {
659			return 0, "", false
660		}
661		raw, ok := obj["value"]
662		if !ok {
663			return 0, "", false
664		}
665		unit, _ := obj["unit"].(string)
666		switch v := raw.(type) {
667		case float64:
668			return v, unit, true
669		case string:
670			if parsed, err := strconv.ParseFloat(v, 64); err == nil {
671				return parsed, unit, true
672			}
673		}
674		return 0, "", false
675	}
676	
677	func parseGateText(text string) (float64, string, bool) {
678		// Take the last matching measurement so "result: 310 ms" still parses.
679		matches := gateMeasurementPattern.FindAllStringSubmatch(text, -1)
680		if len(matches) == 0 {
681			return 0, "", false
682		}
683		m := matches[len(matches)-1]
684		v, err := strconv.ParseFloat(m[1], 64)
685		if err != nil {
686			return 0, "", false
687		}
688		unit := ""
689		if len(m) >= 3 {
690			unit = strings.TrimSpace(m[2])
691		}
692		return v, unit, true
693	}
694	
695	// collectRatchetEvidence flattens the ratchet evidence from each gate result
696	// into a single slice the orchestrator stores on the landing record.
697	func collectRatchetEvidence(gateResults []GateCheckResult) []RatchetEvidence {
698		var out []RatchetEvidence
699		for _, gr := range gateResults {
700			if gr.Ratchet != nil {
701				out = append(out, *gr.Ratchet)
702			}
703		}
704		return out
705	}
706	
707	// summarizeRatchets returns "pass", "fail", or "" (no ratchets evaluated).
708	func summarizeRatchets(evidence []RatchetEvidence) string {
709		if len(evidence) == 0 {
710			return ""
711		}
712		for _, e := range evidence {
713			if e.Decision == "fail" {
714				return "fail"
715			}
716		}
717		return "pass"
718	}
719	
720	// summarizeGates returns the RequiredExecSummary string for the landing result.
721	func summarizeGates(results []GateCheckResult, anyFailed bool) string {
722		if len(results) == 0 {
723			return "skipped"
724		}
725		if anyFailed {
726			return "fail"
727		}
728		return "pass"
729	}

216	// handlePostAttemptDecomposition runs the orchestrator-level splitter when a
217	// no_changes attempt signals orchestrator_action: decompose. It checks the
218	// queue-level max_decomposition_depth (not the implementation prompt cap),
219	// validates the AC map for completeness, and either creates children+deps or
220	// parks the parent for operator review if the split is lossy or depth-capped.
221	// The bead must already be unclaimed before this is called.
222	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) handlePostAttemptDecomposition(ctx context.Context, candidate *bead.Bead, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime, assignee string, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, at time.Time) {
223		emit := func(kind string, body map[string]any) {
224			if runtime.EventSink == nil {
225				return
226			}
227			data, _ := json.Marshal(map[string]any{"event": kind, "payload": body})
228			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.EventSink, "%s\n", data)
229		}
230		parkOperator := func(reason string) {
231			if runtime.Log != nil {
232				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt decomposition blocked: %s (%s)\n", reason, candidate.ID)
233			}
234			emit("post_attempt_decomposition.blocked", map[string]any{
235				"bead_id": candidate.ID,
236				"reason":  reason,
237			})
238			if berr := parkBeadPostIntakeRejection(w.Store, candidate.ID, assignee, PreClaimIntakeOperatorRequired, reason, at); berr != nil && runtime.Log != nil {
239				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt decomposition park error: %v\n", berr)
240			}
241		}
242	
243		// Queue-level depth check: orchestrator uses its own cap, not the
244		// implementation prompt's hardcoded depth-2 cap.
245		maxDepth := rcfg.MaxDecompositionDepth()
246		if maxDepth > 0 && storeBeadDepth(w.Store, candidate) >= maxDepth {
247			overflowBody, _ := json.Marshal(map[string]any{
248				"depth": storeBeadDepth(w.Store, candidate),
249				"max":   maxDepth,
250			})
251			_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
252				Kind:      "triage-overflow",
253				Summary:   "depth cap exceeded during post-attempt decomposition",
254				Body:      string(overflowBody),
255				Actor:     assignee,
256				Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
257				CreatedAt: at,
258			})
259			parkOperator("queue-level depth cap exceeded; operator must split")
260			return
261		}
262	
263		decomp, err := runtime.PostAttemptDecompositionHook(ctx, candidate.ID)
264		if err != nil {
265			parkOperator(fmt.Sprintf("decomposition hook error: %s", err.Error()))
266			return
267		}
268		if decomp == nil {
269			parkOperator("decomposition hook returned no split")
270			return
271		}
272		lossyOrEmpty := isDecompositionLossy(decomp.ACMap) || (len(decomp.ACMap) == 0 && strings.TrimSpace(candidate.Acceptance) != "")
273		if lossyOrEmpty {
274			parkOperator("decomposition AC map is incomplete; operator must produce a lossless split")
275			return
276		}
277	
278		childIDs, decompErr := applyPreClaimDecomposition(w.Store, candidate, decomp, assignee, at)
279		if decompErr != nil {
280			parkOperator(fmt.Sprintf("decomposition apply error: %s", decompErr.Error()))
281			return
282		}
283		if runtime.Log != nil {
284			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt decomposition: bead %s split into %s\n", candidate.ID, strings.Join(childIDs, ", "))
285		}
286		emit("post_attempt_decomposition.applied", map[string]any{
287			"bead_id":   candidate.ID,
288			"child_ids": childIDs,
289		})
290	}

292	// runPostAttemptTriage invokes the optional post-attempt triage hook after
293	// the attempt outcome has been finalized but before any cooldown decision is
294	// applied. Hook failures are fail-open: the report is returned unchanged and
295	// legacy cooldown behavior remains in force.
296	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) runPostAttemptTriage(ctx context.Context, candidate bead.Bead, report ExecuteBeadReport, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime, assignee string, now func() time.Time) ExecuteBeadReport {
297		hook := runtime.PostAttemptTriageHook
298		if hook == nil {
299			return report

300		}
301		if isDeterministicSystemOutcomeReason(report.OutcomeReason) {
302			return report
303		}
304		triage, err := hook(ctx, candidate.ID, report)
305		if err != nil {
306			if runtime.Log != nil {
307				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt triage error (%s %s): %v (continuing)\n", candidate.ID, report.Status, err)
308			}
309			return report
310		}
311		triage = normalizeTriageResult(triage)
312		hasWarnings := len(triage.DecodeWarnings) > 0 || triage.Malformed
313		classificationRecognized := isRecognizedTriageClassification(triage.Classification)
314		if hasWarnings && runtime.Log != nil {
315			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt triage warning (%s %s): %s (continuing)\n", candidate.ID, report.Status, formatTriageWarnings(triage.DecodeWarnings))
316		}
317		if !classificationRecognized && !hasWarnings {
318			return report
319		}
320		if classificationRecognized {
321			report.OutcomeReason = triage.Classification
322		}
323		recordPostAttemptTriageEvent(w.Store, candidate.ID, report, triage, assignee, now().UTC())
324		return report
325	}

327	// CapLoopCooldown clamps a loop-set cooldown duration to MaxLoopCooldown.
328	// The loop uses this for every SetExecutionCooldown call so no automatic
329	// path can silently park a bead for a year.
330	func CapLoopCooldown(d time.Duration) time.Duration {
331		if d <= 0 {
332			return 0
333		}
334		if d > MaxLoopCooldown {
335			return MaxLoopCooldown
336		}
337		return d
338	}

137	// ResolvedConfig.

139	// On load error, LoadAndResolve still returns a usable, sealed
140	// ResolvedConfig populated from package defaults plus the supplied

44	// expectSealedPanic asserts that fn panics with a message naming
45	// LoadAndResolve.
46	func expectSealedPanic(t *testing.T, label string, fn func()) {
47		t.Helper()
48		defer func() {
49			r := recover()
50			if r == nil {
51				t.Fatalf("%s: expected panic, got none", label)
52			}
53			msg, ok := r.(string)
54			if !ok {
55				t.Fatalf("%s: panic value not a string: %T %v", label, r, r)
56			}
57			if !strings.Contains(msg, "LoadAndResolve") {
58				t.Fatalf("%s: panic message %q does not name LoadAndResolve", label, msg)
59			}
60		}()
61		fn()
62	}
